pattern: The number of valid build orders is the count of topological orderings of the rooted tree, which a bottom-up pass computes as a product of multinomial choices over each node's subtree sizes, all under the prime modulus.
hints: Each room depends only on its parent, so the build order is a topological ordering of a rooted tree; count those orderings.
For a subtree of size s with children subtrees of sizes a, b, ..., the orderings interleave the children freely, giving a multinomial coefficient times the product of each child's own count.
Process nodes from the leaves up: ways(u) = (size[u]-1)! / product(size[c]!) times product(ways(c)); compute factorials and modular inverses once.

edge_inputs.description: star: all rooms hang off the root
two independent chains from the root
deep chain then a branch at the tip

stress.description: random rooted tree where each room attaches to an earlier room
deep chain to exercise large subtree sizes
